NVDA.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Cautiously risk-on. S&P 500 at all-time highs (7,200+), April 2026 best month since 2020. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% with 4 dissents (most since Oct 1992) — internal disagreement signals policy uncertainty ahead. Powell transition on May 15 to likely successor Kevin Warsh introduces additional uncertainty. Iran peace talks removed the dominant left-tail oil-spike risk (WTI -2% May 1). Inflation remains elevated but the worst-case energy scenario has eased. Net regime read: risk-on with near-term policy transition overhang. AI capex supercycle narrative intact (Alphabet, Amazon beat, Azure +40%), but MSFT post-earnings selloff and Meta -9% capex shock introduced near-term AI-spend uncertainty — directly relevant to NVDA demand thesis. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is SHORT-TERM underperforming. 5d ROC -4.72%, 10d ROC -1.6% vs broad market risk-on. However, 21d ROC +12.92% shows NVDA led the prior sector upswing. Price broke below EMA-5 and EMA-10, now testing EMA-20/21 confluence — structure is intact at all longer MAs (above EMA-20/50/200) but the recent pullback puts it below short-term averages. Hourly: price is below all hourly EMAs (24, 48, 120) with hourly ADX -DI (36.05) > +DI (12.97) showing active intraday downward momentum. OBV trending down on both daily and hourly — distribution signal, not accumulation. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~$198.45 at EMA-21 / Fib 38.2% confluence zone ($196.75-$197.67 per thesis). Nearest resistance: $203 (hourly pivot, volume-confirmed, 19 tests at $198.70). Target: $209-210 (prior support zone turned resistance, below recent highs). Stop: $193.50 (below Fib 38.2% support at $196.75 plus buffer; a close below the confluence zone invalidates the thesis). Upside: $209 - $198.45 = $10.55 (~5.3%). Downside: $198.45 - $193.50 = $4.95 (~2.5%). Asymmetry: ~2.1:1 — meets minimum 2:1 bar, but barely. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: NVDA fundamentals remain exceptional (61% revenue growth, 144% EPS growth, 59% operating margin, D/E 0.11 near-zero leverage). No balance sheet concerns. ATR 3.23% daily is elevated but consistent with NVDA's norm and the current macro regime. KEY LESSON FROM PRIOR RUN (Apr 30): The last EMA pullback Fib dip-buy at this zone was stopped out at $205.30 (entered too high). The wiki thesis now explicitly identifies $196.75-$197.67 as the real entry zone — current price at $198.45 is AT the identified confluence. However, the prior lesson also warns 'do not enter without bullish confirmation candle.' Hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 is deeply oversold — a potential early sector rotation signal per the prompt — but no bullish hourly candle confirmation is evident yet. OVERALL: Risk-on macro and intact AI thesis support a bullish lean. Price is at the thesis-defined support confluence. Hourly oversold condition suggests bounce potential. However, OBV distribution on both timeframes, ongoing short-term momentum weakness, and the 0% win rate on this exact setup type (EMA pullback Fib dip-buy) warrant reduced confidence. Enter only on a confirmed bullish candle at the confluence, not blindly at current price.
